text.title: Google Ads Reporting Dashboards Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the google-ads-reporting methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every report MUST contain the preset: Impressions, Clicks, CTR, Avg CPC, Cost, Conversions, Conv rate, CPA, Conv value, ROAS, Impression share, Quality Score.
rule.rationale: Diagnostic path requires every column; missing any kills a known lever.

rule.statement: Three views MUST exist: operator weekly (everything), executive monthly (KPI summary + actions), stakeholder summary (1-page).
rule.rationale: Different audiences need different abstraction; one report serves no one.

rule.statement: Every report MUST ship segment breakdowns by device, network, location, time, audience.
rule.rationale: Aggregate hides 2-3x deltas; segment-by-default is the only honest format.

rule.statement: Alerts MUST trigger on: CPA > target +20%, IS < 70%, conv-rate < target -15%, daily-spend > forecast +25%.
rule.rationale: Late detection of these breaches costs 1-2 weeks of budget.

rule.statement: Every report MUST end with a top-5 action queue (priority + lever + expected impact + owner).
